query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election. Here's the summary of key findings:

**Hichilema's expected vote share:** The two most credible polls both show HH commanding — the Namuunda Mutombo national poll (Oct 2025, n=1,009) put him at **60% (58–63%)** vs. 35% for opposition; the academic ZERN survey (fieldwork Jun–Nov 2025) showed **55%** for HH/UPND vs. 13% opposition, with a large 32% undecided bloc. Online straw polls (Zambian Post 55%, Mwamba 52%) concur directionally.

**Edgar Lungu / PF:** Confirmed — Lungu **died 5 June 2025** in Pretoria at 68. He was already barred by term limits (Dec 2024). His burial became an unprecedented legal dispute (Pretoria court ruled for repatriation on 8 Aug 2025, but it dragged into 2026). The PF is **deeply fractured** — a Sampa faction joined the ruling UPND; a Lubinda faction backed Makebi Zulu.

**Opposition coalition strength:** The main challenger is now **Brian Mundubile** (Tonse–Pamodzi Alliance), Lungu's replacement chosen Jan 2026. Other blocs: Fred M'membe's People's Pact (M'membe arrested March 2026) and Harry Kalaba's Orange Alliance. Analysts uniformly describe the opposition as "weakened and disorganised," with fragmentation being the key factor helping HH clear the 50%+1 threshold.

**Turnout factors:** High turnout projected (>82%); 86% of Zambians value elections. But economic grievances (cost of living, unemployment, 72% poor electricity access) and voter apathy among 2021 HH supporters are the main risks. Integrity concerns (gerrymandering, shortened register inspection) also flagged.

The full report and structured data (14 sources, 17 quantitative data points) have been submitted.

key_facts: Zambia's general election is scheduled for 13 August 2026; ~8.8 million registered voters (53.1% women), 14 presidential candidates cleared by the ECZ, 13,529 polling stations across 226 constituencies.
Most credible poll (Dr. Namuunda Mutombo, n=1,009, face-to-face, 2-15 Oct 2025, all 10 provinces, ±3.1%): Hichilema 60% (range 58-63%) vs 35% for opposition combined; 58% say HH kept promises.
Academic Zambia Election Research Network (ZERN/UCT, fieldwork Jun-Nov 2025): 55% intend to vote for HH/UPND, only 13% for an opposition party, 32% undecided or refused.
Online/social-media straw polls concur directionally: Zambian Post Facebook poll HH 55%; Emmanuel Mwamba (opposition-aligned) poll HH ~52%.
Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025 in Pretoria, South Africa, aged 68, from cardiac complications; he was already barred (Dec 2024 ConCourt ruling) from contesting due to term limits, so he is NOT running.
Lungu's burial became an unprecedented dispute; Pretoria High Court ruled 8 August 2025 that his body must be returned to Zambia for a state funeral, but the standoff persisted into 2026.
The Patriotic Front (PF) is deeply fractured: a Miles Sampa faction joined the ruling UPND Alliance (June 2026); a Given Lubinda faction backed Makebi Zulu.
The Tonse Alliance selected Brian Mundubile as its 2026 presidential candidate (Jan 2026 convention) to replace the late Lungu; it merged with PF Pamodzi to form the Tonse–Pamodzi Alliance.
Other opposition blocs: Fred M'membe (People's Pact/Socialist Party, arrested 2 March 2026 under Cyber Crimes Act) and Harry Kalaba (Orange Alliance/Citizens First).
Analysts describe the opposition as 'weakened and disorganised'; fragmentation is the key factor helping HH clear the 50%+1 threshold.
Turnout projected high (>82% per analyst Namukolo Miyanda; 86% of Zambians view elections as best way to choose leaders); HH won 59% in 2021 with strong youth turnout.
Economic grievances are the main drag on HH: ZERN found 41% say personal conditions worsened vs 38% improved; 72% had poor/no electricity access in 2024.

Electoral integrity concerns: gerrymandering allegations (Southern Province seats 20→29), voter register inspection cut 90→14 days; ZERN found only 51% confident elections will be free and fair (62% UPND vs 36% opposition).
